title: NZ School Holidays Travel Tips — How to Beat the Crowds and Save Money
meta_desc: NZ school holidays travel tips to save money and avoid crowds, expert advice from NZFamilyTravel
intro: Travelling during NZ school holidays can be expensive and crowded, but with the right strategies, you can save up to $500 on a family holiday. Peak season prices can be double the off-peak rates, making it essential to plan ahead. For example, a family of four can expect to pay around $250 per night for a motel room in Queenstown during school holidays, compared to $120 per night during off-peak season.

sections.heading: Plan Ahead to Save Money
sections.body: Booking your accommodation and activities well in advance can help you save a significant amount of money. For instance, booking a campsite at Wanaka's Lake Outlet Campground 6 months in advance can cost around $20 per night, compared to $40 per night if booked at the last minute. Similarly, activities like the Skyline Gondola in Queenstown can be booked online for $39 per adult and $25 per child, a savings of $10 per person compared to buying tickets at the door.

sections.heading: Alternative Accommodation Options
list: Consider staying in nearby towns instead of major cities, such as staying in Frankton instead of Queenstown
Look into campervans or motorhomes, which can be rented for around $100-150 per day
Book a holiday home or apartment, which can often be more cost-effective for families than hotels

sections.heading: Free and Low-Cost Activities
sections.body: There are plenty of free and low-cost activities to enjoy in NZ, such as visiting the beach, going for a hike, or exploring a local market. For example, the drive from Auckland to the Coromandel Peninsula takes around 2 hours, and offers stunning scenery and beautiful beaches. You can also visit the Auckland Art Gallery Toi o Tāmaki, which is free to visit and offers a range of family-friendly activities.

sections.heading: Get the Best Deals on Campervans

cta.text: Compare campervan prices and book now

sections.heading: Budgeting for Your Trip
sections.body: Using a budget calculator can help you plan and track your expenses, ensuring that you stay within your means. Our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator is a free tool that can help you estimate costs and make the most of your holiday.

sections.heading: Explore NZ's Hidden Gems
list: Visit the town of Glenorchy, which is around a 45-minute drive from Queenstown and offers stunning scenery and outdoor activities
Explore the Whanganui National Park, which is around a 2.5-hour drive from Wellington and offers beautiful scenery and hiking trails
Take a trip to the Catlins, which is around a 2-hour drive from Dunedin and offers stunning coastal scenery and wildlife viewing opportunities

cta.text: Discover the best destinations for your family holiday
